summary: This week in AI, OpenAI made significant announcements with the launch of GPT-5.6 and ChatGPT Work, while facing legal challenges over trade secrets and copyright issues. Meta removed a controversial AI feature from Instagram and Google will now disclose AI-made ads. Apple sued OpenAI for alleged trade secret theft, and SK Hynix raised $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history.
insight: The increasing integration of AI into various industries is raising important questions about accountability, transparency, and ethics, as seen in the controversies surrounding AI-generated content and the use of AI in decision-making processes.
highlights: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 and ChatGPT Work, expanding its capabilities in AI-powered productivity
Meta faced backlash over an AI feature that generated images based on public Instagram accounts, leading to its removal
Google announced it will disclose which ads are made with AI, enhancing transparency in online advertising

articles.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
articles.description: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

articles.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.description: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."

articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
articles.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.
